overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted professional presence across decentralized and professional networks (LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon) totaling 3,580 followers. Content is highly academic, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. Across all 7 analyzed posts, there is exceptionally high engagement relative to audience size, indicating a deeply invested and relevant audience of peers and researchers.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, tool releases (CooRnet v2.0), and conference announcements focusing on computational methods to detect coordinated social media sharing.
Job listings and research opportunities for Postdoc and PhD candidates in computational social science.
Announcements regarding major milestones such as newly awarded research grants and publication of new academic papers.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high sharing culture (reposts) to amplify recruitment drives and call for papers.
Include direct links to publication repositories or project websites in LinkedIn posts to capitalize on high visibility and drive traffic.
Utilize Mastodon for deep technical discussions and algorithm documentation, which resonates well with its developer and academic community.
Maintain a consistent cross-posting schedule, ensuring tone is adapted (more formal on LinkedIn, conversational on Bluesky and Mastodon).

platformInsights.bluesky: Strongest relative engagement (avg. 6.14% ER). Users are highly collaborative, as shown by high repost-to-like ratios, making it the best platform for spreading news rapidly.
platformInsights.mastodon: Niche but responsive tech-academic audience (avg. 4.55% ER). Very receptive to specific open-source tool updates like CooRnet.
platformInsights.linkedin: Highest absolute engagement volume (avg. 90 interactions per post). Highly effective for professional networking, institutional announcements, and talent recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
